title: GME Soars on Strong Q3 Earnings Beat as Cash Reserves Hit Historic 8.8 Billion
text: GameStop reported adjusted EPS of $0.24, exceeding analyst estimates of $0.16-$0.20. The company achieved its sixth consecutive quarter of profitability, demonstrating a notable turnaround despite a 4.6% revenue decline to $821 million. The company's cash position nearly doubled year-over-year, reaching $8.8 billion compared to $4.6 billion in Q3 2024. With approximately $4.2 billion in long-term debt, GameStop maintains a net cash position exceeding $4 billion, providing significant strategic flexibility. While video game sales declined 27% due to digital shifts, collectibles emerged as a growth engine, representing 31% of revenue. The article notes that this massive war chest has nearly doubled in one year, enabling potential acquisitions or shareholder returns while the company transitions its business model. This strong earnings beat combined with substantial liquidity improvements indicates that GameStop is successfully managing its business transformation while building financial resources for future strategic initiatives.
